title: Toxocarpus lineatus
intro: Toxocarpus lineatus is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Toxocarpus lineatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions and as an antipyretic agent.

Toxocarpus lineatus is a climber or a shrub that grows in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands.
